chunk_summary: This section focuses on linking clinic care to Anganwadi Centers (AWCs) for longitudinal nutrition care, emphasizing documentation, program linkage, and structured follow-up for under-2 children with growth concerns.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of linking clinic care to AWCs for sustained pediatric nutrition support.
Learn documentation practices for anthropometry, program linkage, and escalation decisions in IYCF counseling.
Identify strategies to address poor nutrition outcomes, including household ration diversion and inappropriate preparation.
Apply CMAM follow-up schedules to ensure structured and reinforced care for malnourished children.
key_facts: AWC linkage transforms one-time counseling into longitudinal care for under-2 children.
Document anthropometry using weight, length/height, MUAC, and edema, along with wasting severity classification.
Escalation decisions must involve shared responsibility between caregivers and community workers (AWW and ANM).
Follow-up schedules should align with CMAM guidelines: weekly, fortnightly, or monthly based on severity.
A 9-month-old child on diluted milk and biscuits requires staged transition to energy-dense family foods, hygiene practices, and AWC linkage.
reference_quote: Supplementary Nutrition Rules under ICDS (2017) emphasize structured follow-up and program linkage for pediatric nutrition care.
document_key_topics: Linking clinic care to AWCs for sustained nutrition support
Documentation practices for pediatric nutrition follow-up
Structured follow-up schedules based on CMAM guidelines
